meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your portfolio. Use our advanced SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build, launch, and monetize your MVP in record time.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders don't need a resume, they need a live revenue stream. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you bypass months of boilerplate development. Use AI-driven orchestration to ship a production-ready MVP in days, proving your capability through tangible, scalable product results.
content.intro:
The market doesn't care about your portfolio; it cares about the problem you solve. While you sit idle, obsessing over a lack of past projects, your competitors are shipping MVPs and capturing market share. The 'no portfolio' hurdle is simply a failure to leverage modern tooling to demonstrate immediate value.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'showing' to 'building.' By utilizing a professional-grade SaaS starter kit, you stop writing boilerplate code and start architecting business logic. This is your blueprint for bypassing the gatekeepers and launching a product that stands on its own merits.

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Define your core data models to ensure your SaaS is scalable from day one. Focus on multi-tenancy and user authentication structures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust PostgreSQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for users, organizations, subscription status, and audit logs. Use Prisma syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize Prisma schema with multi-tenant user models and push to local database'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication Flow
steps.description: Security is non-negotiable. Implement OAuth and session management to ensure enterprise-grade security for your initial users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication middleware for Next.js that handles JWT sessions and redirects unauthenticated users to the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure NextAuth.js with Google and Email providers and protect the /dashboard route'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Monetization must be baked into the architecture. Connect Stripe to handle recurring billing cycles and usage-based pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a Stripe webhook handler that listens for subscription.created and subscription.deleted events to update user access levels in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup Stripe API routes and webhook listener for subscription management'

steps.title: Building the Core Value Prop
steps.description: This is where your product differentiates itself. Use AI to automate the primary task your users are paying you to solve.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer that processes user input through an LLM to generate actionable business insights, caching responses in Redis to reduce latency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement AI processing service with Redis caching and error handling'

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Automation
steps.description: Automate your delivery pipeline. Every commit should be tested and deployed to staging without manual intervention.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, unit tests, and deploys the application to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure GitHub Actions for automated testing and Vercel production deployment'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Dollar' over feature parity; if your payment gateway is live, you are already ahead of 90% of developers.
Use feature flags to decouple deployment from release, allowing you to test in production without exposing broken logic to users.
Focus on 'boring' tech stacks; your users care about the solution, not your choice of bleeding-edge database.
Implement automated error tracking (like Sentry) before you launch to catch bugs before your users report them.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a living codebase; refactor aggressively as you learn what your users actually value.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus exclusively on distribution. Run cold outreach campaigns, engage in niche communities, and iterate based on real user feedback loop data.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for your portfolio and start building for your profit. The tools are here, the market is waiting, and your first user is just one deploy away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
